item.title: Inflation inches up in January amid higher food prices: GED
item.description: Inflationary pressure in Bangladesh remained elevated at the start of 2026 as rising prices of fish, fruits and vegetables continued to push up food costs, even though rice prices showed signs of easing, according to the latest report of the General Economics Division (GED). Inflation rose slightl

item.title: Man killed in Narayanganj power struggle
item.description: A man has died in a clash between two groups involved in a power struggle in Narayanganj’s Fatullah. His friend is also undergoing treatment in a hospital and is in critical condition. The incident took place in the Panchabati area around 4:30 am on Thursday, Fatullah Model Police Station ch

item.title: PM cuts use of lights, AC in office room to reduce power consumption
item.description: Prime Minister Tarique Rahman has reduced the use of lights and air-conditioners in his office room in the Secretariat in the interest of saving power. He has also urged all ministries and other organisations to be economical in their consumption of power. Additional Press Secretary Atiqur Rahman

item.title: 50 flights cancelled in Ctg over 6 days
item.description: At least 50 international flights operating between Chattogram and several Middle Eastern destinations have been cancelled over the past six days amid the conflict in the region following the US-Israeli attack on Iran, according to the Shah Amanat International Airport authority. A total of four o

item.title: Govt declares additional holiday on March 18 to ease Eid travel
item.description: The government has declared an additional public holiday on March 18 to facilitate smooth travel for people returning to their hometowns during Eid al-Fitr. As a result, government employees will enjoy a seven-day holiday from March 17 to March 23. The decision to declare March 18 (Wednesday) a ho

item.title: VisionSpring to provide free eye screening for leather sector workers
item.description: To help maintain productivity, skill and quality standards in Bangladesh’s leather, leather goods and footwear industry, international social enterprise VisionSpring has recently signed an agreement with the Leathergoods and Footwear Manufacturers and Exporters Association of Bangladesh (LFME

item.title: TIB calls for cancellation of BB governor appointment
item.description: Transparency International Bangladesh (TIB) has called for the cancellation of the appointment of the governor of Bangladesh Bank (BB) to safeguard the central bank’s independence and neutrality, ensuring it remains free from any potential conflict of interest. Speaking at a conference, Ifte

item.title: BNP delegation meets US envoy Kapur
item.description: A BNP delegation has met US Assistant Secretary of State for South and Central Asian Affairs S Paul Kapur. The meeting was held at the home of the US ambassador to Bangladesh in Gulshan on Thursday, according to Shairul Kabir Khan, a spokesman for the ruling party. The delegation entered the resi

item.title: US-Bangla flight brings home 378 passengers stranded in Dubai
item.description: A first US-Bangla Airlines special flight has brought 378 Bangladeshis who were stranded in the United Arab Emirates amid the Iran conflict back home. The passenger included 27 crew members of national flag carrier Biman Bangladesh Airlines, US-Bangla said in a statement on Thursday. US-Bangla Ai

item.title: 210 flights cancelled at Dhaka airport
item.description: A total of 210 flights have been cancelled at Hazrat Shahjalal International Airport till Thursday following airspace closures by Iran, Iraq, Kuwait, the UAE, Bahrain, Qatar, and Jordan due to the escalating security situation in the Middle East. The flight cancellations over the past few days are

item.title: IRGC claims it hit US tanker in Persian Gulf
item.description: The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps announced that its naval forces struck an American oil tanker in the northern Persian Gulf early Thursday, setting it ablaze, reports Mehr News Agency (MNA). In a statement, the IRGC's public relations department said the vessel was hit by missiles and is curr

item.title: Thieves steal 200 bhori of gold, silver at Khilgaon jewellery shop
item.description: Burglars broke through the wall of a jewellery shop at Khilgapn in Dhaka and allegedly fled with gold and silver jewellery on Wednesday night. Officer-In-Charge of Khilgaon Police Station, Md Shafiqul Islam, said the shop is situated on the ground floor of a residential building, reports UNB. &ld
